steps.title: Identify the slope
steps.content: The equation $l = 30 + 2w$ is linear in slope-intercept form. The 2 is the coefficient of $w$, so it is the slope of $l$ versus $w$.

steps.title: Interpret the slope in context
steps.content: Slope = (change in dependent variable) / (change in independent variable). Here $l$ (length, cm) depends on $w$ (weight, N), so the 2 means $l$ increases by 2 cm for each 1 N increase in $w$.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A) Length with no weight — that's the y-intercept 30, not the 2.
  • B) Weight that stretches the spring 30 cm — nonsense for the coefficient 2.
  • C) Weight increase per 1 cm of length — that would be the reciprocal slope $\tfrac{1}{2}$ N per cm, not 2.
  • D) Length increase per 1 N of weight — matches the slope of 2 cm per N exactly.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
